subject: [SO] Release the H release for sol005 adapter
commit_message:
[SO] Release the H release for sol005 adapter

Rename the vfc-adapter to etsi-sol005-adapter.

Issue-ID: SO-3473
Signed-off-by: seshukm <seshu.kumar.m@huawei.com>
Change-Id: I79949521c58b05f9b7eacad591d06f62e3ab1983

Patch Set 3:

Sounds like a bug fix and not a new feature so we should proceed. Nevertheless Seshu you should not track all the bug fixes as a task but as separated bugs

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Sounds like a bug fix and not a new feature so we should proceed. Nevertheless Seshu you should not track all the bug fixes as a task but as separated bugs

IMHO, it's clearly not a bugfix:
* SO build for this component has changed
* name has changed
* repository has changed (moved from "so main repo" to its own repo)

After, as said to Seshu, SO is under refactoring. 
Clearly, this specific one has been forgotten when renaming into OOM (which was started before M3) has started.

So as long as (or as soon as ;) ) this patch fulfills the "gating rules" (100% Healthcheck except full, 100% e2e tests), it will be merged.

> 
> After, as said to Seshu, SO is under refactoring.

Thanks Sylvain, yes this is not a defect fix but part of the so plan.
As is communicated we have this on hold as the core components needs to be merged first.
I have updated the SO issue number to the relevant one for better understanding.

Patch Set 4:

I had a sync-up with Seshu. I understand that the word "issue" did not mean "bug"(Sorry). So I have renamed the SO-3566 in alignment with the purpose of this ticket a.k.a. Refactoring
